sections.title: Pre-quest
Talking to Queen Sigrid before the quest

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...Hello, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Have you come for a particular reason? I am a busy Queen, you know. I had to attend the opening of a new orphanage this morning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: And this afternoon I shall be receiving the Embassador of Karamja and accompanying him on his week-long tour of Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Then this evening we shall be putting on a grand ball! All of the kingdom's nobility will be present!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I m...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Oh, who am I kidding...nothing ever happens here. I always wanted to be a princess when I was a girl. If I'd been told it would be like this, I wouldn't have bothered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Yes? Did you want something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...actually, no.

sections.title: Talking to Guard at door to throne room

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Halt! Who goes there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My name is [Fremennik name], will you not let me through?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm afraid the King won't give an audience to anyone except a worthy hero.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: If you become a member of the Heroes' Guild, he will definitely grant you an audience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a member of the Heroes' Guild; does that not show my worth?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Then you may pass.

sections.title: Talking to Guard at the throne room after entering once

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: [Welcome back, Sir/Welcome back, Madam/Welcome back, ].

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Your Royal Highness.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim before the quest

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, Sir/Greetings, Madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: The King needs someone to take over the rule of the Kingdom. If I were you, I should consider the position.

sections.title: Getting started
Talking to King Vargas

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Who are you? Who seeks audience with the King of this fair land of Miscellania?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am known in these lands as [Fremennik name], Your Majesty. I come in search of a quest.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Ah, it pleases me to see such enthusiasm!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I was once an adventurer such as yourself; even while fulfilling my duties as King of Miscellania, I would roam the lands in search of adventure, and strike down mighty foes with a single blow!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Unfortunately, I was a little too daring, and attempted to brave the icelands, where I finally met my match.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I survived, barely, but I was cursed for the rest of my life to stay in this form. Which leaves me incapable of performing my duties as ruler.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: This is why I am looking for an exceptional soul to be regent of this fair kingdom of Miscellania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I may be so bold, Your Majesty, I think Miscellania would rest in capable hands with me.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Hahaha! You are bold, [Fremennik name], but then I have yet to see a good ruler who was not.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: You will first have to demonstrate your worth. You are a good warrior, that I do not doubt; however ruling also requires a good grasp of diplomacy.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: We have been at war with neighbouring Etceteria for hundreds of years; however, I think both of us have rather lost our appetite for hostilities.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Now may be an opportune time to make amends. If you manage to negotiate peace with them, then you will truly have shown your skill as a diplomat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then do not stray far, Your Majesty, for I shall soon return.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Wait! There is one more matter to discuss... a mere formality, of course.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: By Miscellanious tradition, one may not become regent of the kingdom unless in the trust of the royal family.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: This includes that of the future king or queen. It is yet too early for me to decide which of my children should inherit the throne.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Until then, gaining the trust of one of them should suffice. Who knows? Your choice might help me make a decision later.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: So tell me! Which of my children will you attempt to gain the trust of?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would like to choose Prince Brand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you say this is a mere formality, Your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I am sure you can form a good working relationship with him. You can ask Ghrim for advice; that's what he's here for, after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would like to choose Princess Astrid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you say this is a mere formality, Your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I am sure you can form a good working relationship with her. You can ask Ghrim for advice; that's what he's here for, after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I must decline, Your Majesty. The call of adventure is too strong for me to accept a sedentary life.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I can assure you that I managed to heed that same call, and ruling this land was no hindrance to me. However, I understand your decision. Fare thee well!

sections.title: Talking to King Vargas again after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: How are you getting on, [Fremennik name]? Have you talked to Queen Sigrid of Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to earn Prince Brand's approval instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: As you wish. Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I changed my mind.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: As you wish. I will keep this conversation between us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to earn Princess Astrid's approval instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

sections.title: Talking to King Vargas after starting to gain approval

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: How are you getting on, [Fremennik name]? Have you talked to Queen Sigrid of Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to earn Prince Brand's approval instead.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Is that so? I can let them both know, but you may find that the Prince has taken the news rather coldly. A broken trust is difficult to mend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to earn Princess Astrid's approval instead.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Is that so? I can let them both know, but you may find that the Princess has taken the news rather coldly. A broken trust is difficult to mend.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, Sir/Greetings, Madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Negotiating peace with Etceteria, Sir/Negotiating peace with Etceteria, Madam/Negotiating peace with Etceteria].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make peace with Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You should go talk to Queen Sigrid of Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I supposed to gain the trust of the Prince?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: I think you may find that talking to him would be a good way to start.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: A ring may be an appropriate gift to formalise your friendship

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I supposed to gain the trust of the Princess?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: I think you may find that talking to her would be a good way to start.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else you can tell me?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: What would you like to know, [Sir/Madam/ ]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the King?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: King Vargas was once a brave and noble adventurer, well-loved by his subjects. Unfortunately, he was cursed while seeking a quest in the icelands to the north, and is now unable to reign.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Prince?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Prince Brand has always dreamed of becoming a famous bard; unfortunately, his musical and literary skills are somewhat lacking.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: He has no interest in ruling the kingdom, and seems to spend his time daydreaming or composing quite awful poems, and sagas of totally mundane events.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: He is rather vain, if you'll pardon my saying so; he will greatly appreciate any compliments on his poetry. He also has quite a sweet tooth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Princess?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Princess Astrid thinks she'd be perfectly capable of ruling the kingdom, but her father doesn't trust her enough by herself.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Especially since she devotes herself to the - as the King sees it - cowardly skill of archery rather than the noble skill of melee combat...

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: She is fond of jewellery, and of course she collects bows. She is also quite the conversationalist.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Many have tried to capture her attention, but she has yet to be impressed by any of them.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Many have tried to capture her attention, but you are the first to succeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Queen Sigrid?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Queen Sigrid is quite envious of King Vargas, since he has the larger kingdom.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: The King and she have never seen eye-to-eye, but I think if you approach her you may find her quite reasonable.

sections.title: Giving a ring to Prince Brand too early

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I couldn't possibly accept this.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Brand

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: [Good day, sir/Good day, madam/Good day].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As I'm sure you are aware, your father the King is seeking someone to rule the Kingdom in his stead. I have put myself forward, but in order for me to become ruler...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: ...you have to earn my approval?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Ahahahaha!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Come now, what interest would I have in a crass adventurer like yourself? What would you know of the poetry that flows through my veins like an avalanche through a peaceful mountain village?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you may find I know good poetry when I see it, Your Royal Highness...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: This may be the case. I am willing to give you a chance; if you impress me enough, I will consider trusting you as our regent.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Brand again after formal introduction

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, why did you decide to become a bard?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Poetry and music have always fascinated me. I am drawn to them like a moth to an overenthusiastic mage who has set himself on fire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How poetic.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Why thank you. Sometimes I amaze even myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a strange metaphor...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: It is a simile, but what do you know of poetry?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, are you the moth or the mage?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: The moth!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I composed a saga, would you like to hear it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know if I have time for a whole saga...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Don't worry, it's quite short. Here we go!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Once upon a time there was a Prince, And Brand was his name, He always had a song in his heart, And a rhyme in his head,

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: And he became the world's greatest bard, And delighted one and all, With his songs and sagas, And Brand was his name!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How inspiring!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Thank you! I thought I would compose something for future generations, to tell them my story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think my ears are bleeding.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: If you don't appreciate my singing, you are free to leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not a saga!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: It's a short one, I'll admit...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you do if you became ruler of Miscellania?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Oh, I would never concern myself with such trivial matters as those involved in ruling a Kingdom. My interests lie solely in the arts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A much nobler pursuit, to be sure.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I am glad you agree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If only you were any good at them...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I am insulted, [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're just afraid of responsibility.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Perhaps you are right...

lines.text: How are you getting on, [Fremennik name]? Have you convinced Sigrid yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, Your Majesty.

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid after King Vargas asks to change the anthem

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hello. Have you talked to Vargas yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have, Your Majesty. He says he will recognise the country when you change your anthem.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: What? But it's a great anthem, I composed it myself! Listen!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: "You're stupid, we're the best, Etceteria beats the rest! and Queen Sigrid is very pretty, King Vargas is a fool, hey, nonny-nonny!"

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid he insisted, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Well, it can't be helped I suppose...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: You'll have to find a bard to write a new one though. And there isn't a bard to be had for love nor money in Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do, Your Majesty.

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid again after she asks for a new anthem

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hello. Have you found a new anthem yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, Your Majesty.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim after Sigrid asks for a new anthem

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, Sir/Greetings, Madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make peace with Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You should find someone to compose a new anthem for Etceteria.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Brand to ask for a new anthem

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need someone to compose a new anthem for Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: You've come to the right bard! I'll write one for you immediately!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can take your time, really...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: No problem, I'm already done! Here, listen!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: 'Etceteria, Etceteria, Oh what a great country it is, Etceteria, Etceteria, So I wrote a song about it.'

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: 'It's somewhere in the sea, And it looks very nice, If it weren't for your ancestors, It wouldn't be there at all,'

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: 'And when things aren't going too well, We sing this lovely song, And then we'll all feel so much better, It won't take very long.'

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Here's a copy, you can keep it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...that's lovely, thanks.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: [Don't mention it, dear/Don't mention it, sir/Don't mention it, madam/Don't mention it].

sections.title: Talking to Prince Brand after losing the anthem

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost my copy of the *ahem* theme you composed...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Fear not! I made many, many more!

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Here's a copy, you can keep it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...that's lovely, thanks.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: [Don't mention it, dear/Don't mention it, sir/Don't mention it, madam/Don't mention it].

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid after getting the awful anthem

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hello. Have you found a new anthem yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Your Majesty, but I'm afraid it isn't very good.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Let's see now...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: 'Etceteria, Etceteria, Oh what a great country it is, Etceteria, Etceteria, So I wrote a song about it.'

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: 'It's somewhere in the sea, And it looks very nice, If it weren't for your ancestors, It wouldn't be there at all,'

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: 'And when things aren't going too well, We sing this lovely song, And then we'll all feel so much better, It won't take very long.'

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Oh dear, no, this simply won't do. Find a better one.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim with the awful anthem

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, sir/Greetings, madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I got Prince Brand to compose a new Etceterian anthem for me, but it's awful!

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Oh dear. Well, that is only to be expected. May I see it, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Hmm, yes...allow me to make a few adjustments.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [There you go, sir/There you go, madam/There you go]. Are there any other matters in which I may be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make peace with Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You should get another copy of the anthem.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim with the good anthem

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, sir/Greetings, madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make peace with Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You should show the anthem to Queen Sigrid.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim after losing the good anthem

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, sir/Greetings, madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the corrected anthem you gave me.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Do you have another copy of the prince's anthem for me to correct?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here it is.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Hmm, yes...allow me to make a few adjustments.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [There you go, sir/There you go, madam/There you go]. Are there any other matters in which I may be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have one.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: If you bring me another, I can correct it. Are there any other matters in which I may be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make peace with Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You should get another copy of the anthem.

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid with the good anthem

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hello. Have you found a new anthem yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Let's see now...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: 'Etceteria, Etceteria, Of our country we are proud! Etceteria, Etceteria, Let all sing its name out loud!'

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: 'Rising high above the water, The jewel of the North! Our mothers and our fathers, To build this land came forth!'

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: 'And in the times of darkness, Sing praises of our land! And in our hearts we can be sure, Our victory is at hand!'

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Why, this is quite good! I'm surprised!

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Here is a treaty declaring what we've mutually agreed to. I've already signed, so all it needs is King Vargas' signature and it'll be official.

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid after getting the treaty

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hello. Has Vargas signed the treaty yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Hello. Has Vargas signed the treaty yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I lost it, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: How silly of you. I'll make you another.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim after getting the treaty

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, sir/Greetings, madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make peace with Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You should show the treaty to the King.

sections.title: Talking to King Vargas after getting the treaty

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have here a treaty awaiting your signature.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I doubt I will be able to hold an ordinary pen well enough to sign the treaty. You will have to find a pen large enough for me to hold.

sections.title: Talking to King Vargas after he asks for a pen

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: How are you getting on, [Fremennik name]? Have you found me a pen yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, Your Majesty.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim after King Vargas asks for a pen

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, sir/Greetings, madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make peace with Etceteria?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You should find a way to make the nib and body for a very large pen.

sections.title: Talking to Derrik after King Vargas asks for a pen

lines.speaker: Derrik
lines.text: Good day, [Sir/Madam/ ]. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a slightly strange request...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The King needs to sign a treaty, but is unable to because he lacks a pen large enough for him to hold. Could you make me a nib of the right size?

lines.speaker: Derrik
lines.text: Hm... I may be able to manage that. What sort of size?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the size of a helm should do it.

lines.speaker: Derrik
lines.text: All right, but I'll need an iron bar to make it from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have one with me.

lines.speaker: Derrik
lines.text: Well, come back when you have one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have one here.

lines.speaker: Derrik
lines.text: Good. Just a minute...

lines.speaker: Derrik
lines.text: Here you go! One giant pen nib!

sections.title: Using the giant nib on logs
Talking to King Vargas after getting the giant pen

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: How are you getting on, [Fremennik name]? Have you found me a pen yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here it is, Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Excellent! And now, with a flourish, I sign the end of the rivalry between our two proud kingdoms!

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Now, how's it going with my [son/daughter], will they accept you as regent?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [He/She] will, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Excellent, excellent.

sections.title: Talking to King Vargas after he signs the treaty

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Ghrim tells me that the population is not yet very supportive of the idea of having you as ruler. You should try to gain their support.

sections.title: Gaining public support
Talking to Advisor Ghrim about becoming regent

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Greetings, sir/Greetings, madam/Greetings].

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: I should tell you right away that killing the people of this Kingdom will not make you popular with anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: [Gaining the support of the population, sir/Gaining the support of the population, madam/Gaining the support of the population].

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Currently, [number]% of the population supports you.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: This is enough for you to become Regent; I should talk to King Vargas if I were you.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: You will need 75 % of the population in your support in order to become Regent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I gain the support of the population?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: King Vargas would often go and help out his subjects in their daily tasks, when he was still able to do so, and they loved him dearly for it.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Currently, [number]% of the population supports you.

sections.title: Talking to King Vargas after gaining enough support

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Ghrim tells me that the population would be glad to have you as a ruler.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I see no reason to delay your regency any further. Ghrim, please proceed with the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Yes, Your Majesty, I will proceed at once. Ahem...

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: We are gathered here today to witness the rise of a new hero to the regency of the Kingdom of Miscellania, to continue the work of our great King Vargas who is unfortunately now no longer able to rule.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: By the powers invested in me by the Miscellaneous Constitution, I declare thee, [Fremennik name], to be the new Regent of this fair kingdom.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: May you bring success and prosperity to Miscellania and to all of its people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...is that all?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: That is all. I wish you luck as Regent. Speak to Ghrim if you require assistance.

sections.title: Post-quest
Talking to King Vargas after the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: That fool Sigrid! What does she think she's doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: Ah, [Fremennik name]. I apologise, but I'm rather busy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on, your Majesty?

lines.speaker: King Vargas
lines.text: I'm busy, [Fremennik name]. You'd better talk to Ghrim.

sections.title: Talking to Princess Astrid when Prince Brand is the heir

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Good day, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Princess Astrid.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Brand when he is the heir and not married

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: How fares the kingdom?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All is well, my Prince

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Prince Brand, will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Oh, my [darling/dear], you've made me the happiest person alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that a yes?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Yes! We'll have to put off the actual ceremony until we can find a priest, but you can tell my father I have agreed to marry you.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Brand when married to him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you found a priest for our marriage yet?

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I'm afraid not, dear. I don't think there is one to be had on these islands for love nor money...

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Do not fear, though; I shall call for you as soon as one is found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm having second thoughts about the wedding. This marriage would change things, with an increase in responsibilities and duties you were keen to avoid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While I want to support the kingdom, I don't want that to end up impacting your life negatively. Maybe it's too soon to make this kind of decision.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: You're right, I am not interested in more responsibilities. I would have less time for poetry! Is that your final decision?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I think it's for the best.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: I think you'll make a great Thane for our kingdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe not. It it hard, for I am so very fond of you. Let's leave it as it is for now and see what the future holds.

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Agreed.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Brand when Princess Astrid is the heir

lines.speaker: Prince Brand
lines.text: Good day, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day, Prince Brand.

sections.title: Talking to Princess Astrid when she is the heir and not married

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: How fares the kingdom?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All is well, Princess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Princess Astrid, will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Oh, my darling, you've made me the happiest person alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that a yes?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Yes! We'll have to put off the actual ceremony until we can find a priest, but you can tell my father I have agreed to marry you.

sections.title: Talking to Princess Astrid when married to her

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you found a priest for our marriage yet?

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I'm afraid not, darling. I don't think there is one to be had on these islands for love nor money...

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Do not fear, though; I shall call for you as soon as one is found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm having second thoughts about the wedding. This marriage would change things, with an increase in responsibilities and duties that would overshadow the activities you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While I want to support you and the kingdom, I don't want that to end up impacting your life negatively. Maybe it's too soon to make this kind of decision.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: My darling, that is very thoughtful of you and you make a convincing point. Are you... absolutely sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I think it's for the best.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: I think you'll make a great Thane for our kingdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe not. It it hard, for I am so very fond of you. Let's leave it as it is for now and see what the future holds.

lines.speaker: Princess Astrid
lines.text: Agreed.

sections.title: Talking to Advisor Ghrim after the quest

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Greetings, Your Royal Highness.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Greetings, Thane [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is the Kingdom faring?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Your subjects are worried about the possibility of war, but they are still happy to work for you.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Your current approval rating is 75%. I'm afraid your subjects have not yet collected anything.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: This is how your subjects are distributing their effort.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has anything been happening in the kingdom recently?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I manage the kingdom?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Your loyal subjects work hard to collect resources for you. Every day they bring what they have collected to the castle. The resources which they collect are coal from the rocks to the north, maple logs from the trees

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: to the east, herbs from the garden just outside the castle, and tuna and swordfish fished at the port to the south. They are paid from the money in the coffers; each day, one-tenth of the money in the coffers is

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: distributed to them. A maximum of 50k is paid to your subjects and the coffers can store up to 5 million gp. Another thing which affects the amounts of resources collected is your approval rating. The more your

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: subjects love you, the more they will be motivated to work hard. King Vargas would often go and help out his subjects in their daily tasks, when he was still able to do so, and they loved him dearly for it. Finally, you

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: can choose how much of their efforts your subjects devote to each resource; of course, the harder they work at collecting one, the less time they have to spend collecting the others.

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Have you got a copy of my book? It explains everything in more detail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't...

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Here, you can have this one, I have plenty spare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Dealing with all the little troubles that come up from time to time, Thane [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Advisor Ghrim
lines.text: Such as the predicament we are now in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has anything been happening in the kingdom recently?

sections.title: Talking to Queen Sigrid after the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your Majesty...

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Vargas, you irritating fool. 'Warmongering harpy', indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: Oh, I apologise. I was merely thinking out loud.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is your kingdom faring?

lines.speaker: Queen Sigrid
lines.text: It could be better. Now, if you don't mind, I'm rather busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, your Majesty.
